column3: A hypervisor is a virtual machine manager.
column4:
Distinction

This card tests the acronym. Hypervisor purpose is tested with a Basic card.

column4:

column3: It runs on top of an existing host operating system.
column4:
Why

Type 2 classification is a common hypervisor distinction.

column2: In VM networking, {{c1::NAT}} lets the VM share the host's network connection.
column3: NAT converts between the VM's private address and the host's network address.
column4:
Distinction

Bridged networking places the VM on the physical network. Private networking keeps the VM inside an internal virtual network.

column3: VDI provides remote desktop sessions or applications to client devices.
column4:
Why

The acronym is explicit desktop virtualization scope.

column2: Containers are lightweight partly because they use the host operating system's {{c1::kernel}}.
column3: A full VM normally runs its own guest operating system.
column4:
Distinction

This card tests compact container architecture recall. The container-versus-VM comparison is tested with a Basic card.
